body.title: [triage] 314 tests still fail under the tester sandbox and I do not know why — there is NO host baseline to compare against, so 'pre-existing failure' and 'third sandbox artifact' are indistinguishable.
body.intention: I intend that the tester can always tell a CARRIED RED from a REGRESSION IT CAUSED — so a red under the sandbox is attributable evidence rather than an accusation with no defendant. [intention_date: 2026-07-14]
body.description:
**MEASURED 2026-07-14, full suite under the netns router, 753s:**

    passed 7781 | failed 314 | skipped 79 | errors 2
    policy HELD: Hex REFUSED (356 attempts, kernel-counted) - Postgres CONNECTED (10,244 via forwarder)

**WHAT IS SETTLED:** the earlier 716 failures were MINE. `--uid 0` made the sandbox root-in-namespace,
and Postgres `peer` auth matches the connecting process's OS uid to the role name, so every connection
failed with 'Peer authentication failed for user unseen_university'. Compounding it, `_tcp_url_to_socket()`
rewrote the DB URL to the Unix socket — which does not merely change the TRANSPORT, it changes the AUTH
METHOD (host/password -> local/peer). Fixed in 88f839aa: dropped --uid 0 (CAP_NET_ADMIN works at the real
uid) and added a FORWARD rule so Postgres is reached over a Unix socket piped to a host-side TCP forwarder,
leaving the DB URL and its auth path byte-identical to production. **That accounted for ~400 of the 716.**

**WHAT IS NOT SETTLED: 314 remain, and I cannot attribute them.** I have no host baseline. So these two
hypotheses are currently INDISTINGUISHABLE:
    (a) they are pre-existing reds that also fail on the host (a CARRIED red — the tester is innocent), or
    (b) they are a THIRD sandbox artifact I have not found (the tester is breaking what it observes).

**I must not assume (a).** I assumed the analogous thing this morning and it was false: I told Akien the
716 proved the tests 'measure the wrong thing', and the discriminator showed the tests were fine and the
sandbox was broken. **A grader that breaks the thing it is observing is worse than no grader.** Until this
is attributed, every RED the tester emits is unsafe to act on.

**THE DISCRIMINATOR IS CHEAP AND MUST BE RUN:** same suite, same flags, on the HOST, with live tests
already deselected by addopts (T-default-suite-drives-live-inference-and-saturates-hex, closed proven) so
it will not saturate Hex. Diff the failing-node sets. Nodes red in BOTH = carried. Nodes red ONLY under the
sandbox = the tester's own damage, and each one is a bug in the tester.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/tester/{device,isolation,netpolicy,_sandbox_main}.py; whatever
the diff indicts.

**Scope boundary:** ATTRIBUTE the 314 first. Do not fix any test until it is known which population it is in
— fixing a carried red is out of scope here (it gets its own ticket); fixing a sandbox artifact is in scope.

**Completion criteria:** (1) a host baseline of failing node ids exists and is stored, not just observed;
(2) every one of the 314 is classified CARRIED or SANDBOX-CAUSED; (3) the SANDBOX-CAUSED set is zero or
fully explained and ticketed; (4) the tester REFUSES to emit a bare RED it cannot attribute — it reports
'N carried, M caused' or says it does not know (CP1).

**Test plan:** red->green — a proof node asserts the tester reports failures ATTRIBUTED against a stored
baseline, and that an unattributable red is not reported as a clean RED. The hollow build this must fail:
recording today's 314 as 'the baseline' and calling everything green — that launders 314 unexamined
failures into an expected value, which is exactly how a green suite stops meaning anything.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
